item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from this video with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoops. I intended that bit to imply that Rostam never actually applied for a higher salary - she was just making a point that she was losing far more to the project than she thought she had to gain, and was not properly informed of the risks prior to her application due to the intentional incompetence of Pollock. Hopefully I've cleared that up with an extra sentence.

Oh, and given that her consultancy isn't a mandatory directive from the higher-ups, as Driver was sort of an illicit side-project, I'd say she's perfectly justified in handing in her resignation when she's at risk of losing her sanity (and lord knows what else).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Foundation agent who thinks they can just walk off projects or demand salary negotiations because of mental health issues.

Ha! No. Suspension of disbelief completely shattered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…it wasn't clear to me that the plate numbers assigned to unconventional vehicles were previously assigned to conventional vehicles, and that is an important point to get across.

To quote the second sentence of the description:

In cases of SCP-2942, existing South African license plate numbers and NaTIS registration numbers are assigned to vehicles which are not recognised as conventional motor vehicles…

Edit: I added a "re-" in there to improve clarity in that regard. Thanks for the heads-up.

I used to do volunteer work at an aquarium that housed some pretty big spider crabs; the things are durn weird. An old myth goes that they're the transmogrified spirits of child-emperor Heike's samurai army, who chose to commit suicide by drowning rather than surrender to their sworn enemy. Then National Geographic ran a story on the bleak future of India's rickshaw pullers.
Dice, splice and what do you get? Headcrabs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that the Description of the effects could be made clearer. I was initially thrown off by "the legal owner of a vehicle which has had its registration number re-assigned becomes physically incapable of operating their original vehicle", because it wasn't clear to me that the plate numbers assigned to unconventional vehicles were previously assigned to conventional vehicles, and that is an important point to get across. And there are a few other points where I wasn't sure whether "vehicle" referred to -1 or -2 instances.

That said, the Japanese spider crab prevented me from downvoting, and the characterisation of -3 led me to upvote. Nice work in developing this story away from the simple starting point into something richer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, reading drafts in chat sure makes going through the most recently created pages a breeze! :V +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, this is the strange that keeps me coming back. The 'more complicated than originally believed' remark in the footnotes was just the cherry on top. Or disembodied hand, as the case may be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent skip- tried to upvote it three or four times while I was reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was so weird. At first I thought it was trying a bit too hard to be strange, but it tied up nicely. Definitely original.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got hooked at the caption and kept that interest throughout the rest of the article. Good work, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted the minute I saw the caption.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really nice. Any plans for an accompanying tale? That last collapsible made me want to know more about the -3s

+1

item.title: Quite Enjoyable
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed the article, though I found it a little hard to follow along. Great job though, keep it up!
